title: Pilosella magnauricula
taxonomy: Pilosella magnauricula is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification places Pilosella magnauricula in the order Asterales, which includes other flowering plants such as coneflowers and chrysanthemums. Within the Asteraceae family, Pilosella magnauricula is a member of the subfamily Cichorioideae. It is also a member of the genus Pilosella, which is a group of small to medium-sized plants often characterized by their white or yellow flowers. Pilosella magnauricula is specifically a member of the Pilosella section of the genus.

intro: Pilosella magnauricula is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), specifically a type of hawkweed that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to promote wound healing, reduce pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella magnauricula include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
